data.title: EastGroup Properties: Strong Fundamentals and Development Outlook Underpin Buy Rating Despite Near-Term FFO Headwinds
data.description:
BMO Capital analyst John Kim has maintained their bullish stance on EGP stock, giving a Buy rating on April 13.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

John Kim has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including EastGroup Properties’ strong first-quarter performance and solid growth outlook for 2026. He highlights robust cash same-store NOI expansion, an FFO per share beat with full-year guidance raised, and higher expected development starts, all supported by healthier pre-leasing levels and improving rental rates.

He also emphasizes the company’s healthy balance sheet, with leverage metrics remaining conservative and occupancy still high despite a modest projected decline later in the year. While noting that near-term FFO guidance lags consensus and that development yields have moderated versus recent history, he views these as manageable issues within an overall positive fundamental story that supports further share appreciation.

In another report released on April 13, Wells Fargo also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $232.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Definium Therapeutics: Strengthened Investment Case and Multi-Indication Upside Ahead of Key Phase 3 Readouts
data.description:
Needham analyst Ami Fadia maintained a Buy rating on Definium Therapeutics today and set a price target of $28.00.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ami Fadia has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, primarily the strengthening investment case following Definium Therapeutics’ recent investor day. She now views DFTX more favorably, highlighting it as the firm’s top pick ahead of several pivotal Phase 3 data readouts for DT120 ODT across major depressive disorder, generalized anxiety disorder, and an additional key study later in 2026.

The rating also reflects increased confidence in the clinical program’s robustness, including extended 12‑week efficacy and durability data from EMERGE and significantly enhanced statistical power in the PANORAMA GAD trial after a favorable sample‑size re‑estimation. Furthermore, management’s decision to expand DT120 ODT into post‑traumatic stress disorder with a Phase 3 trial planned for 2027 underscores a broader, multi‑indication revenue opportunity that supports a constructive long‑term outlook on the stock.

According to TipRanks, Fadia is a 5-star analyst with an average return of 30.5% and a 57.00% success rate. Fadia covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Praxis Precision Medicines, Atai Beckley N.V., and Biogen.

In another report released today, RBC Capital also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $36.00 price target.

data.title: Lululemon: Leadership Transition Uncertainty Justifies Neutral ‘Hold’ Rating
data.description:
Needham analyst Tom Nikic has maintained their neutral stance on LULU stock, giving a Hold rating on April 15.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Tom Nikic has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors tied to Lululemon’s leadership transition and investor expectations. The appointment of longtime Nike executive Heidi O’Neill as CEO brings sector-relevant experience, particularly in women’s athletic wear, but the market had been hoping for Jane Nielsen, a highly regarded operator, which has weighed on the stock’s immediate reaction.

Nikic also notes that O’Neill’s most senior tenure at Nike coincided with a controversial strategic period, which may initially temper investor confidence. Given that major turnarounds in the athletic space typically unfold slowly and O’Neill will not assume the role for several months, he believes it is too early to turn more positive or negative on the shares, supporting a neutral, Hold stance for now.

According to TipRanks, Nikic is a 4-star analyst with an average return of 8.9% and a 51.53% success rate. Nikic covers the Consumer Cyclical sector, focusing on stocks such as On Holding AG, Carter’s, and Crocs.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Boston Scientific: Sustained Outperformance and Solid Growth Support Buy Rating Despite Trimmed Target
data.description:
Michael Matson, an analyst from Needham, maintained the Buy rating on Boston Scientific. The associated price target was lowered to $77.00.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Michael Matson has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors including Boston Scientific’s continued revenue and EPS outperformance versus consensus despite a reset in 2026 guidance. He notes that while organic growth moderated in the first quarter as FARAPULSE and WATCHMAN expansion decelerated and Urology and CRM encountered operational pressures, overall growth remains solid on an absolute basis and above many peers.

Looking ahead, Matson acknowledges that management anticipates further slowing in FARAPULSE and WATCHMAN but expects stabilization and improvement in Urology and CRM to partially offset these headwinds. He also factors in the temporary nature of gross margin pressure from tariffs and POLARx inventory charges, and while he meaningfully trims his price target to $77 from $97 to reflect a more conservative growth and margin trajectory, he still sees attractive upside from current levels, which supports maintaining a Buy recommendation.

In another report released today, Barclays also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $100.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
